title: Lotononis densa
taxonomy: Lotononis densa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Lotononis, which is native to southern Africa. Lotononis densa is a type of shrub or small tree that is commonly found in dry areas. The classification of Lotononis densa is further broken down into its kingdom (Plantae), phylum (Magnoliophyta), and class (Rosopsida). This classification indicates its botanical relationship to other plants within the Fabaceae family.

Traditionally, it is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. There is limited scientific research on the plant's medicinal properties, but its traditional uses have been documented in various African cultures. intro: Lotononis densa, commonly known as the "tree lucerne" or "yellow lotononis," is a perennial shrub native to southern Africa. This plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and infections. The bioactive constituents of Lotononis densa include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
